mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations. A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the preceding 3 months predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through this valuation channel.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment signals robust labor demand and consumer income growth, which sustains corporate revenue and profit expectations. When PAYEMS increases by more than 150k persons month-over-month, S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days turn positive due to improved earnings visibility.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in < 55% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) reflects consumer spending strength and forward economic momentum. When RSAFS decelerates by more than 2% month-over-month, it signals weakening demand that compresses equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 50% of instances where RSAFS decelerates > 2%, or the mechanism fails to predict drawdowns in 2 consecutive recession episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization and supply-side health. When INDPRO declines for two consecutive months, it signals supply-side weakness that reduces profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in < 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture consumer and construction demand. When both HOUST and RSAFS grow above their 12-month moving averages simultaneously, it signals broad-based demand strength that drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction reflects coordinated consumer and business confidence.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1168k, RSAFS at 509025m as of 2024-02-15
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in < 58% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power (R² increase < 0.01) beyond single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate jointly determine real monetary conditions. When CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annualized while FEDFUNDS remains below 2.5%, it signals monetary accommodation relative to inflation, creating negative real rates that support equity valuations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 251.846, FEDFUNDS at 1.91% as of 2024-02-15
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with above-target inflation
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in < 55% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, which compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This threshold marks the transition from tight to slack labor conditions.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 52% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the mechanism fails to predict equity drawdowns in 2 consecutive recession episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1), payroll employment (PAYEMS), and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly measure aggregate demand momentum. When all three series show positive quarter-over-quarter growth simultaneously, it signals broad-based economic expansion that sustains corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The interaction captures synchronized demand across consumption, investment, and employment.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth > 0% AND PAYEMS MoM growth > 0 AND RSAFS MoM growth > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18514.595B, PAYEMS at 149279k, RSAFS at 509025m as of 2024-02-15
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in < 60% of jointly triggered instances, or the three-way interaction term adds no incremental predictive power (R² increase < 0.015) beyond pairwise interactions.
